extracted.Courses: Algebra I and Bridge to Algebra
extracted.Data sources: Answer Sequences with Length of Answer
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: BKT models only consider a finite set of parameters per knowledge component without considering learner-specific parameters.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
BKT with student parameters uses negative log likelihood as the objective function.

In essence, some of the BKT parameters are specific for each student.
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Authors extract student specific features that are used in the models: A priori knowledge of skill and learning rate of student which has impact on the probability of transitioning a state from unknown to known.
extracted.Comments:
The authors also have 2 skill models. 

Authors find that adding student-specific probability of learning is more important than probability of initial mastery.
extracted.Limitations:
Only BKT is explored. 

Limityed number of datasets.

Does not consider student-specific versions of 2 parameters: pSlip or pGuess

annotations.text: Bayesian Knowledge Tracing is popular method in Intelligent-Tutoring Systems that uses skill-specific parameters, but often fails to account for student variability. Thus, the authors propose an improvement to BKT which hinges on the introduction of student-specifc parameters.

Results show that the introduction of these parameters greatly improves the prediction capabilities of BKT on new students. In addition, they also show that parameterizing speed of learning also beats parameterizing a-priori knowledge.
